body.title: Provenance service: rack-issued identity tokens at announce
body.result: ProvenanceService issues HMAC-signed tokens at announce; stored in registry/tokens.json; returned in Manifest.token; wired into Skeleton + AnnounceBroker; 12 tests green
body.description:
Agents currently self-report their identity at announce. The rack accepts whatever agent_id/instance they claim. A provenance service issues a signed token at announce time — not self-reported, rack-verified. Every subsequent action by that agent carries the token.

Token contains: agent_id, instance, announce_timestamp, rack_signature. Stored in flat-file registry alongside the device manifest. Token is short-lived (expires at deregister or rack restart). Other services (memory writes, channel posts, tool calls) can verify the token against the registry.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/announce/broker.py, unseen_university/announce/manifest.py, ~/.unseen_university/registry/ (token field added to registry JSON)
**Design rules:** flat-file registry survives DB outage; no Postgres dependency in announce path
**Scope boundary:** IN — token issuance at announce, token stored in registry, token returned in manifest; OUT — cryptographic PKI (HMAC shared secret is sufficient for v1), revocation, cross-rack federation
**Completion criteria:** announcing agent receives a token in its manifest; registry JSON contains the token; a second announce with the same agent_id but no valid prior token gets a fresh token (not reuse)
